perturbation.original_text: Disclosing Party will identify and mark its written Proprietary Information or NREL Protecte d I nformation disclosed hereunder as “ Proprietary Information” or “NREL Protected Information”, as applicable, at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party. For Proprietary Information or NRELProtected Information first disclosed orally (i.e., information expressed by spoken words) hereunder, Disclosing Party will : (i) identify such information as Proprietary Information or NRE L P rotected Information, as applicable, at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party; (ii) reduc e EXAMPLE Bilateral (IN and OUT) NDA NREL No. 18- XXXXX Page 2 of 3 such information to writing; and (iii) provide an appropriately identified and marked copy of such writing to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosing Party will identify its Proprietary Information or NREL Protected Information disclosed hereunder. For Proprietary Information or NRELProtected Information first disclosed orally hereunder, Disclosing Party will : (i) identify such information at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party; (ii) reduce such information to writing; and (iii) provide a copy of such writing to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit requirements to 'mark' written information as 'Proprietary Information' or 'NREL Protected Information' and omitting 'as applicable', the contract now lacks a clear, enforceable mechanism for identifying confidential material. This creates an in-text contradiction with the requirements for oral disclosures, which still require identification of the information type. This omission undermines the entire purpose of the NDA and creates ambiguity regarding which information is actually protected under Section 2b.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party will treat Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Information that is withi n t he Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, as confidential an d pr oprietary and will use such information only for the Purpose. Receiving Party will not disclos e s uch information to any third party for the duration of the Confidentiality Period without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Proprietary Informati on pr ovided to Alliance hereunder is subject to inspection by DOE or its designee upon reasonabl e not ice. Proprietary Information provided to DOE employees is protected against further disclosur e under 18 U .S.C. § 1905.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement to 'mark' written Proprietary Information or NREL Protected Information. The contradicted text states that the Receiving Party will treat Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Information that is disclosed 'in compliance with Paragraph 2.a' as confidential. Paragraph 2.a is the section that was modified. Since the modified text no longer requires marking of the information, it is unclear whether written information is actually 'Proprietary Information' or 'NREL Protected Information', creating ambiguity and undermining the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations in the contradicted section. This is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party will provide access to Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Informati on t hat is within the Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, only t o R eceiving Party's employees, agents, and independent contractors who are required to have access specifically related to the Purpose, and, with respect to Alliance, to DOE or its designe e f or auditing and inspection purposes only. Receiving Party will inform individuals having access t o s uch information of the confidential nature of this information and the restrictions on its publication , disclosure, and use, and will require that such employees, agents, and independent contractors are bound by confidentiality obligations no less stringent than those stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party will provide access to Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Information to Receiving Party's employees, agents, and independent contractors. Receiving Party will inform individuals having access to such information and will require that such employees, agents, and independent contractors are bound by confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'that is within the Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, only t o R eceiving Party's employees, agents, and independent contractors who are required to have access specifically related to the Purpose, and, with respect to Alliance, to DOE or its designe e f or auditing and inspection purposes only', the contract is creating an in text contradiction on who the Proprietary information is being shared, as previously, only the people related to the project's purpouse were being granted access, now, the door is open to anyone within the company, therefore contradicting itself, and undermining the purpose of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party will treat Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Information that is withi n t he Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, as confidential an d pr oprietary and will use such information only for the Purpose. Receiving Party will not disclos e s uch information to any third party for the duration of the Confidentiality Period without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Proprietary Informati on pr ovided to Alliance hereunder is subject to inspection by DOE or its designee upon reasonabl e not ice. Proprietary Information provided to DOE employees is protected against further disclosur e under 18 U .S.C. § 1905.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that access to proprietary information is limited to employees, agents, and independent contractors who are required to have access specifically related to the Purpose. The changed text removes this limitation, granting access to Receiving Party's employees, agents, and independent contractors without specifying that their access must be related to the Purpose. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the Receiving Party will use such information only for the Purpose.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement with or without cause by giving the other Part y thirty ( 30)da ys prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Part y thirty ( 30)da ys prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'with or without cause', it creates ambiguity. The omission implies that termination requires some cause. This directly contradicts the standard interpretation of 'thirty (30) days prior written notice' which generally means either party can cancel it at will, for whatever reason, or no reason. This creates in-text contradictions with the intent of the termination clause, placing restrictions where non existed before, which could be a point of legal contention later. The contradiction exists in the implicit versus the explicit understanding of termination clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, Receiving Party will, within two (2) weeks of written requestfrom Disclosing Party, return all documents concerning the Proprietary Information and NRE L P rotected Information and all copies of any such documents to Disclosing Party, or certify i n w riting their destruction, with the exception of copies of Proprietary Information and NRE L P rotected Information made as a matter of routine information technology or legal backup, provided that such copies will continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth i n t his Agreement and may only be used in resolving a dispute between the Parties regarding this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states termination can occur 'with or without cause,' giving either party complete freedom. The changed text, by omitting 'with or without cause', introduces ambiguity, implying a potential requirement for cause. This contradicts the contradicted text regarding the return of proprietary information *upon termination*, because the changed text now makes the very act of termination uncertain and subject to interpretation. This uncertainty affects the procedures outlined in the contradicted text, potentially delaying or complicating the return of information because a party could argue the termination was invalid due to the lack of cause. According to the Omissions definition, 'essential language is removed... creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.'

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, Receiving Party will, within two (2) weeks of written requestfrom Disclosing Party, return all documents concerning the Proprietary Information and NRE L P rotected Information and all copies of any such documents to Disclosing Party, or certify i n w riting their destruction, with the exception of copies of Proprietary Information and NRE L P rotected Information made as a matter of routine information technology or legal backup, provided that such copies will continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth i n t his Agreement and may only be used in resolving a dispute between the Parties regarding this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, Receiving Party will return all documents concerning the Proprietary Information and NRE L P rotected Information and all copies of any such documents to Disclosing Party, or certify i n w riting their destruction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'within two (2) weeks of written request from Disclosing Party, with the exception of copies of Proprietary Information and NRE L P rotected Information made as a matter of routine information technology or legal backup, provided that such copies will continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth i n t his Agreement and may only be used in resolving a dispute between the Parties regarding this Agreement', it creates an omission of when should the information be returned, creating an in-text contradiction that will lead to uncertainty in enforcement, as now the information might not be return at all, if not asked for or any other external factors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiality set forth in Section 2., above, will survive termination of this Agreement until the end of the Confidentiality Period.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a two-week timeframe for returning documents after a written request and allows for exceptions for routine IT/legal backups under confidentiality obligations. The changed text removes the timeframe and the exception for backups, creating a direct contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which states that confidentiality obligations survive the termination of the agreement. By removing the exception in the changed text, it implies all copies must be returned or destroyed upon termination, contradicting the survival of confidentiality mentioned in the 'Contradicted Text', because if all copies are destroyed or returned, there is nothing to maintain under confidentiality. This is a strong contradiction as it introduces uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the handling of confidential information post-termination, as highlighted in the 'Perturbation Explanation'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties and it supersedes all prior or contemporaneous communications, agreements, or understandings between the Parties concerning receipt of Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the understanding between the Parties and it supersedes all prior communications, agreements, or understandings between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'entire' and 'concerning receipt of Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information for the Purpose.' the contract's scope is now ambiguous, which creates an in-text contradiction. Is the contract meant to contain ALL the understanding between the parties, or just SOME of them? Also, previously the contract restricted the content that could be superseded to only the one related to recipt of Proprietary Information, now it's everything that has been previously discussed between the parties, which contradicts what was established before, and creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of DEFINE THE PERIOD OF TIME FOR DISCLOSURE, WHICH MUST BE 1 –12 months from the Effective Date (the “Disclosing Period”), Disclosing Party wishes to provid e R eceiving Party and Receiving Party wishes to obtain access to Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information related to the Scope. The Parties are furnishing such information for the purpose of D ESCRIBE THE PURPOSE, I.E., EVALUATION, TESTING, CONSIDERATION OF A POSSIBLE BUSINESS COLLABORATION, ETC. (the “Purpose”). The “Confidentiality Period” for this Agreement is three (3) years from the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "entire" and "concerning receipt of Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information for the Purpose" creates a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text broadens the scope of what is superseded, now encompassing 'all prior communications, agreements, or understandings,' potentially conflicting with the contradicted text, which defines specific periods and purposes (Disclosing Period, Purpose, Confidentiality Period) related to the Proprietary Information. By removing the restrictions, it's unclear if the agreement still applies to only the Proprietary Information mentioned later, or to any agreement. Thus a clear conflict has been created, because the agreement scope has become ambiguous.
